title: Paneer Butter Masala

subs.name: firm tofu (pressed and drained) for a dairy-free version; reduce simmer to 3 minutes
ghee for a more aromatic and traditional finish
2 tbsp almond paste (badam paste) as a thickener alternative
3 tbsp coconut milk stirred in at the end for a lighter, dairy-free option

steps.text: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai over medium flame. Fry the onion with cardamom, cloves, cinnamon and bay leaf until the onion is soft and translucent. Add the cashews and ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes, then add the tomatoes with a pinch of salt. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes completely break down and oil separates, about 10 minutes.

Take the pan off the heat, cool for 5 minutes, then blend the entire mixture until completely smooth in a mixie or blender. Press the puree through a fine-mesh sieve, pushing with the back of a spoon; discard seeds and fibrous skins. Rinse the blender with 50 ml water and add it to the strained sauce.
Melt 2 tbsp butter in the same kadai over medium flame. Pour in the strained sauce and cook, stirring often, until it darkens slightly and butter pools at the edges, about 5 minutes.

Add the red chilli powder, half the garam masala and the sugar if using. Stir for 30 seconds, then pour in 100 ml hot water. Season with salt and simmer 3 minutes — the sauce should be thick enough to coat a spoon.
Add the paneer cubes and stir gently to coat. Simmer on low flame for 5 minutes so the paneer warms through and absorbs the sauce; do not boil vigorously or the paneer will toughen.

Rub kasuri methi between your palms to release aroma and add it to the pan along with the remaining butter, cream and garam masala. Stir, simmer 2 more minutes, then taste for salt. Serve immediately with naan, butter roti or rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: paneer → firm tofu (pressed and drained) for a dairy-free version; reduce simmer to 3 minutes; cream → 3 tbsp coconut milk stirred in at the end for a lighter, dairy-free option; cashew → 2 tbsp almond paste (badam paste) as a thickener alternative; butter → ghee for a more aromatic and traditional finish
